originalTitle: Women secretly filmed, then ridiculed and abused online
title: Let's Share with Care: Always Ask First Online!
content:
Hello, my wonderful African friends! It's your friendly news reporter here, ready to share something important with you. Today, we're going to talk about sharing. You know how much fun it is to share your toys, your snacks, or your stories with your friends? Well, sharing can be lots of fun, but we also need to remember to share in a kind and careful way, especially when it comes to pictures and videos of people.

Sharing Videos: A Story from Kenya and Ghana

In two amazing African countries, Kenya and Ghana, some grown-up women had a bit of a surprise. They told a big news company, the BBC, about what happened. These women met a man from a far-away country called Russia. This man took videos of them with his camera or phone. But guess what? He shared these videos on the internet for everyone to see. And he did it *without asking them first* if it was okay. That's a very important part of the story. How do you think that made these women feel? It might make them feel sad, or confused, or even a little bit upset. Because when someone shares your picture or video without asking, it's like they are taking something that belongs to you – your image – without your permission. Imagine if someone took your favourite toy and showed it to everyone without asking you. You might feel a little surprised or unhappy, right? It's kind of like that with pictures and videos of people.

Why Is Asking Permission So Important?

Asking for permission is super important, my friends! It means you are being respectful. Respect means treating people with kindness and understanding their feelings. Imagine you have a super cool drawing you made. Your friend loves it and wants to show it to everyone in class. What should your friend do first? They should ask you, right? "Can I show your drawing, please?" If you say "yes," that's great! If you say "no," that's okay too. It's your drawing, and you get to decide. It's the same with videos and pictures of people. Grown-ups and children all have the right to decide if their faces or voices are shared online. It's called their privacy, and it's very special. When we ask someone, "Is it okay if I take your picture?" or "Is it okay if I share this video of you?" we are showing them respect. We are telling them that we care about their feelings and their choices. This is a very important rule for being a good friend, both in real life and on the internet.

Did You Know? Fun Facts About Our World!

* **Did you know?** Kenya is a beautiful country in East Africa! It has amazing animals like mighty lions, huge elephants, and tall giraffes walking in the savannah. You can even see Mount Kenya, which is the second-highest mountain in all of Africa! It's a wonderful place to visit and learn about nature. * **Did you know?** Ghana is another wonderful country, on the west side of Africa! It's very famous for its delicious cocoa beans, which are used to make yummy chocolate! Mmm, chocolate! Ghana also has beautiful beaches and a rich history. * **Did you know?** The internet is like a giant, invisible playground that connects computers and phones all over the world! It helps us learn new things, talk to friends and family far away, and watch fun videos. But just like a real playground, we need to be safe, kind, and respectful when we are on the internet.

Being Kind and Safe Online

The internet is a wonderful place, full of fun things to see and learn. But just like in real life, we need to be good friends and good neighbors when we are online. That means always being kind with our words and with the pictures and videos we share. It also means thinking before we click or share anything. Before you ever share a picture or a video of someone else, even your best friend or a family member, always remember to ask them first. It's a simple question: "Is it okay if I share this?" If they say "no," then that's their choice, and we must respect it. We only share when everyone says "yes, please!" This helps everyone feel safe and happy online.

What Can YOU Do?

You, my amazing little reporters, can help make the internet a happier and kinder place for everyone! Always remember the "Ask First" rule. It's a superpower for online safety and kindness! If you're ever taking a picture or video of a friend or family member, remember to ask them: "Is it okay if I take your picture?" And if you want to share it online, ask again: "Is it okay if I put this online for others to see?" If you ever see someone sharing a picture or video of you or someone else without asking, it's okay to tell a grown-up you trust. You can tell a parent, a teacher, or another trusted adult. They can help you understand what to do and make sure everyone is safe and respectful. Remember, your feelings are important, and so are the feelings of others. Let's promise to always share with care and make our online world a happy and respectful place for everyone in Africa and all over the globe! Keep being kind and smart!
summary: Women in Kenya and Ghana had their videos shared online by a man without their permission, teaching us the very important lesson that we must always ask someone if it's okay before we take or share their picture or video, to be kind and respectful online.
whyMatters: This matters because everyone, big or small, has the right to decide if their pictures or videos are shared. We need to be kind, respect other people's choices, and always ask for permission to make sure everyone feels safe and happy, especially on the internet.

originalTitle: South Africa's exclusion from G7 summit no surprise, says Ramaphosa
title: Our President and the Big Meeting!
content:
Hello, Friends! Welcome to African Kids News!

Hello, my wonderful friends! I am your news reporter, and I am here to tell you a very interesting story. It's about our amazing continent, Africa, and a big meeting that happened far away. We are going to learn about important leaders and how they talk to each other. Get ready for some fun learning!

What is a Big Meeting?

Imagine your school. Sometimes, teachers and the principal have a big meeting, right? They talk about important things for all the students. They talk about what snacks to have, what games to play, and how to make school better. Well, leaders of countries also have big meetings! They meet to talk about very important things for all the people in the world. They talk about how to keep everyone safe. They talk about how to help people who need food or medicine. They talk about sharing good ideas. **Did you know?** These big meetings help countries work together like a team! It's like when you play soccer with your friends – you all work together to win!

Meet the G7 Group

This big meeting we are talking about is called the G7 Summit. "G7" is short for "Group of Seven." It means there are seven very important countries that usually meet. These countries are like big brothers and sisters of the world. They are America, Canada, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, and the United Kingdom. Wow, that's a lot of important countries! They meet to discuss big plans for the whole world. They want to make the world a better place for everyone. **Did you know?** The G7 leaders often talk about how to make sure everyone has food and water. They also talk about keeping our planet clean and healthy!

Our Amazing President Ramaphosa

Now, let's talk about someone very special from our own home, Africa! We have a wonderful leader in South Africa. His name is President Cyril Ramaphosa. He is the President of South Africa. He works very hard for all the people in South Africa. He helps make sure children can go to school. He helps make sure people have jobs. He is a very important person for our country and for Africa. He represents all of us. **Did you know?** South Africa is a very big country right at the bottom of our continent, Africa! It is known for its beautiful nature and friendly people. President Ramaphosa cares deeply about our continent.

An Invitation!

Guess what? Our President Ramaphosa was invited to the big G7 meeting! This was a very happy thing. It meant that the other big countries wanted to hear what our President had to say. They wanted to hear ideas from Africa. France was the country hosting the meeting. France wanted President Ramaphosa to come and share his thoughts. This was a good chance for Africa to speak at a very big table. **Did you know?** When a country gets invited to such a big meeting, it means they are seen as important. It means their voice matters a lot!

Oops! An Un-Invitation!

But then, something a little bit sad happened. Our President Ramaphosa was *un-invited*. Can you imagine? It's like you invite a friend to your birthday party, but then later you have to tell them they can't come anymore. This is called "rescinding an invitation." It means taking back the invite. So, President Ramaphosa could not go to the G7 meeting anymore. This was a surprise to some people. **Did you know?** Being un-invited can sometimes feel a bit confusing. But it happens sometimes, even with important people.

A Powerful Voice from America

Who made this happen? Well, it seems a very powerful leader from a country far away was involved. This leader is President Trump. He was the President of America at that time. America is one of the biggest and most powerful countries in the world. President Trump had a very strong voice. **Did you know?** America is also called the United States. It's a country with many different people and very tall buildings!

Why Did This Happen?

Reports say that President Trump told France something important. He told France that if President Ramaphosa came to the G7 meeting, he (President Trump) would *not* come. This is like saying, "If *that* friend comes to the party, I won't come!" This is called a "threat to boycott." To "boycott" means to refuse to go somewhere or do something. President Trump put pressure on France. "Pressure" means telling someone very strongly what you want them to do. So, France had a difficult choice to make. They had to choose between President Trump coming or President Ramaphosa coming. **Did you know?** Sometimes, even big leaders have disagreements. It's like when friends have a small argument, but much bigger!

Our President Was Not Surprised

When President Ramaphosa heard this news, he said he was "not surprised." He knew that sometimes, big countries make decisions like this. He understood that these things can happen in the world of big politics. He said that Africa must continue to work together. He said that Africa must make its own plans and keep growing strong. Even without being at that specific meeting, Africa's voice is still very important. **Did you know?** President Ramaphosa always thinks about how to make Africa stronger and louder on the world stage. He wants all African countries to shine!

What Does This Mean for Africa?

This story tells us that Africa sometimes faces challenges. But it also tells us that Africa is strong! Our leaders, like President Ramaphosa, keep working hard for us. They keep trying to make Africa's voice heard. Even if one door closes, another one will open. We must always believe in ourselves and our continent. We must learn to stand tall and work together. We will continue to build a bright future for all children in Africa! **Did you know?** Africa is a continent with 54 amazing countries! Each one is special, but together, we are even stronger.

Let's Learn Together!

So, my young friends, we learned a lot today! We learned about big meetings, important leaders, and how countries talk to each other. We learned that even when things don't go as planned, it's important to stay strong and keep working. Always remember that your voice matters too! Keep asking questions, keep learning, and keep dreaming big for Africa! Goodbye for now!
summary: Our leader from South Africa, President Ramaphosa, was invited to a big meeting of important countries called the G7, but then he was un-invited after the American President said he would not come if our President was there.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows that leaders from Africa sometimes face challenges, but it also reminds us that Africa is strong and our leaders work hard to make our voices heard on the world stage.

originalTitle: Rationing power and diluting petrol - how African countries are coping with effects of Iran war
title: Africa's Big Challenge: Making Fuel Last!
content:
Hello, Super Reporters!

Welcome to our special news report for young explorers across Africa! Today, we're going to talk about something really important that affects our cars, our lights, and even the buses that take us to school. Don't worry, we'll explain everything in a super simple way! Sometimes, things happen far away that can make a difference right here at home. We're going to learn about a challenge that many African countries are facing and how clever people are working to solve it.

What is This 'Oil' Juice?

Imagine a special kind of thick, dark liquid that comes from deep, deep under the ground. We call this 'oil.' It's like super food or juice for many of our machines! This oil is turned into things like petrol for cars and buses, and diesel for big trucks that bring food to our shops. It also helps make electricity for our homes and schools. So, oil is super important because it helps everything move and makes our lights shine bright. It's the energy that keeps many parts of our world running. Did you know? People sometimes call oil 'black gold' because it's so valuable and helpful for so many things!

A Problem Far, Far Away

Now, there's a big problem, like a big quarrel, happening in a country very far away called Iran. This fight isn't near us, but it's making it harder for the world to get enough of that special oil juice. It's like a big traffic jam for the ships that carry oil across the oceans. Because of this big problem, it's difficult for many countries, including some in Africa, to buy all the oil they need. This means there's less oil to go around for everyone, and sometimes it becomes more expensive too. It's like when there are not enough sweets in the shop, and they cost more!

Less Oil, More Worries

When there is less oil, it causes what we call 'shortages.' A shortage means there isn't enough of something that people need. So, if there's an oil shortage, it means there isn't enough petrol for cars, not enough diesel for trucks, and sometimes, not enough fuel to make all the electricity we want. Imagine if the buses can't get enough fuel to take you to school, or if the big trucks can't bring fresh vegetables to your market. It can make things a bit tricky. Sometimes, this also means the lights might go off for a little while in our homes or schools. This is why many people are thinking hard about how to solve this.

Sharing Our Power (Rationing)

One clever way some African countries are trying to cope is by 'rationing' power. What does 'rationing' mean? Imagine you have a yummy cake, but you want to make sure everyone in your family gets a piece. You cut it into smaller pieces and share it out fairly. That's a bit like rationing. When countries ration power, it means they share out the electricity. Sometimes, this means the electricity might be turned off in certain areas for a few hours. This is done so that other areas can have power, and so that the power station doesn't run out of fuel completely. It helps make sure everyone gets some electricity, even if it's for a shorter time. Did you know? The sun and the wind can also make electricity! These are called 'renewable' energy sources because they never run out.

Making Fuel Last Longer

When there isn't enough petrol or diesel, countries and people try to find ways to make what they have last longer. This is very important. Sometimes, some people might try to mix petrol with other liquids to make it seem like there is more. But this is not a good idea! It can hurt cars and make them stop working. It's much better to save fuel carefully. Instead of doing things that can damage engines, smart people are thinking about how we can all use less fuel. This could mean walking to nearby places instead of driving, or making sure car journeys are planned well so no fuel is wasted. Every little bit of saving helps make the fuel last longer for important things like ambulances or food trucks. Did you know? A bicycle uses zero fuel, just your strong legs! Riding a bike is a fun and healthy way to save fuel.

How African Countries Are Being Smart

African countries are full of very clever people who are working hard to solve this challenge. They are looking for new ways to get oil from different places around the world. They are also thinking about long-term solutions, which means finding new and exciting ways to make power. Many countries are investing in making more electricity from the sun (solar power) and from the wind (wind power). They are also exploring power from water (hydro power). These kinds of energy don't need oil and are much cleaner for our beautiful planet. It's like finding new recipes for energy!

What Can We Do?

Even kids like you can help! Here are some simple things you can do to help save energy and fuel: * Turn off the lights when you leave a room. * Turn off the TV or radio if no one is watching or listening. * If it's safe, walk or cycle to places close by instead of asking for a car ride. * Remind your parents gently to save fuel and electricity too. * Be understanding if the lights go off sometimes. Remember, it's about sharing!

A Bright Future!

It's true that there's a challenge right now, but Africa is strong and full of smart people. Our countries are working together to find the best solutions. By saving energy, trying new ways to make power, and helping each other, we can make sure our future is bright and full of energy! Thank you for being such great listeners, super reporters!
summary: Many African countries are facing oil shortages because of a problem far away, leading them to share electricity and find smart ways to save fuel, while also looking for new energy sources like sun and wind.
whyMatters: This is important because oil helps our cars move, brings food to our homes, and makes electricity for our lights and schools. When there's less oil, things can get harder, but by being smart and saving energy, we can help keep everything working and find new ways to power our lives.

originalTitle: Iran emergency workers search for survivors after deadly US-Israel attacks
title: Brave Helpers in Iran: A Story of Caring and Hope
content:
Hello, Little Superheroes!

Hello, my amazing friends! It's your kid-friendly reporter from Africa here, ready to share some important news with you. Sometimes, things happen far away that can make us feel a little bit sad, but also show us how brave and kind people can be. Today, we're going to talk about some very special helpers in a country called Iran.

What Happened Far Away?

Far, far away from us, in a big country named Iran, some sad problems have been happening. Imagine when you and your friends might argue, but sometimes grown-ups have bigger arguments. These big arguments have caused some troubles and made things very difficult for many people. It’s like when a big storm causes a lot of mess, but these troubles are from fighting.

Who Are the Emergency Workers?

When big problems happen, like a house getting broken or people needing help, very special people come to the rescue! We call them emergency workers. They are like real-life superheroes, but without capes! They wear special clothes and have special tools to help others. Did you know? Emergency workers are doctors, nurses, firefighters, and even people who help clear up broken things! They are always ready to jump in and help when someone is in trouble. They work very hard to keep everyone safe and sound. They are very brave and kind.

Searching for Friends

These amazing emergency workers in Iran are very, very busy right now. They are searching for people who might be stuck or hurt after these sad problems. Imagine playing hide-and-seek, but instead, these grown-ups are looking for people who need a helping hand. They look everywhere, under things, and around things, to make sure no one is left behind. They use their strong hands and their clever minds to find everyone. It's a very important job because every person is special. They want to make sure everyone is safe. They don't stop until they have looked in every single place they can.

A Place Called Qom

In Iran, there is a city called Qom. It is a big city, just like some big cities we have here in Africa. In Qom, some very sad things happened because of these big problems. Some families in Qom are feeling very, very sad right now. Did you know? Qom is an ancient city, meaning it has been there for a very, very long time! It has many beautiful buildings and a lot of history. Even in old cities, people sometimes face new problems, but they always try to help each other.

Sad News and Big Numbers

It is very sad to hear, but in Qom, six people lost their lives. This means they are no longer with us. This makes many hearts ache with sadness. And not just in Qom, but in all the places where these problems have been happening, many, many people have lost their lives. The grown-ups say that almost 2,000 people have lost their lives in total because of these big problems. That's a very, very big number. Imagine almost 2,000 kids in your school. That's how many people. It is a very sad count.

Why Do People Help?

Even when things are very, very sad, there are always kind people who want to help. Why do they do it? Because it is important to care for each other. When someone is hurt or sad, we want to make them feel better. That’s what emergency workers do. They show us how much people care. They help because every life is precious. Just like your teachers care for you, and your parents care for you, emergency workers care for strangers. They want to bring smiles back to sad faces and make sure everyone gets the help they need.

Heroes All Around

These emergency workers in Iran are truly heroes. They work day and night, even when they are tired, to help others. They show us that even in tough times, there is always hope and kindness. We can all be heroes in our own ways too! Did you know? In many parts of Africa, we also have amazing emergency workers who help people during floods, fires, or even when someone is lost in the bush! They are everywhere, helping their communities.

Being Kind, Wherever You Are

This story from Iran reminds us that being kind is super important. We might not be able to help the emergency workers in Iran, but we can be kind right here, right now. We can help our friends, share our toys, or listen when someone is sad. Every little act of kindness makes the world a little bit brighter. It helps chase away some of the sadness. Even a smile can make a big difference to someone having a bad day.

Learning to Care

So, what can we learn from our friends in Iran? We learn that people are brave. We learn that people care for each other a lot. And we learn that even when sad things happen, there are always helpers. Let’s remember to be thankful for all the helpers in the world. And let’s try to be a helper ourselves, every single day. Because when we help each other, we make the world a better, happier place for everyone.
summary: Brave emergency workers in Iran are searching to help people after sad problems, reminding us that kindness and caring for others are very important.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows us that even when bad things happen, there are always brave people who help others, and it reminds us to be kind and care for everyone around us, no matter where they are from.

originalTitle: How extensive is Russia’s military aid to Iran?
title: Friends Far Away: Russia, Iran, and Their Special Sharing!
content:
Hello from Your Friendly African Reporter!

Hello, amazing friends! It’s your friendly news reporter here, all the way from our wonderful continent of Africa! I hope you are having a super day, full of smiles and learning. Today, we are going to learn about two countries that are very far away from us. These countries are called Russia and Iran. Did you know that there are so many countries in the world? Just like we live in a country here in Africa, there are countries all over the Earth! And sometimes, these countries talk to each other and even help each other out. It's just like how you help your friends at school.

Meet Russia and Iran

Imagine a very big map of the world. Russia is a super, super big country, one of the biggest in the whole world! Iran is another country, also quite big. They both have their own people, their own stories, and their own special ways of doing things. For a long, long time, Russia and Iran have been talking and working together. Sometimes, countries become like friends. They share things and help each other. Today, we're talking about how Russia helps Iran with some very special things.

What is "Helping" for Countries?

When we talk about countries helping each other, it's not like sharing a toy at playtime. For countries, 'helping' can mean sharing special equipment. This special equipment is often used by their armies. Armies are groups of people who protect their country and keep everyone safe. We call this kind of help 'military aid'. It sounds like a big word, but it just means one country gives another country things that help its army. It’s like when you give a friend a special tool to help them fix something. Russia and Iran have been doing this for a very long time.

Sharing Special Tools

What kind of special things do countries share for their armies? Well, they can be things like big airplanes that fly really high in the sky. Or strong tanks that can drive over bumpy land. Sometimes, it's special boats or other important items that help soldiers do their job. Did you know that these 'weapons' are not for playing? They are very serious tools used by grown-up soldiers to protect their country and its people. Russia has often given Iran some of these special tools. It's like Russia is giving Iran some strong hammers and saws to help build a very strong fence around its home. They have exchanged these things over many years. This means Russia gives Iran some things, and maybe Iran gives Russia some other things in return, or they just help each other out because they are friendly.

Eyes in the Sky!

Now, let's talk about something super exciting! Have you ever looked up at the night sky and seen the stars? Way, way up there, even higher than airplanes, there are special machines called satellites. These satellites are like big, shiny eyes that float around our Earth. Did you know these satellites help us with many things? They help us watch the weather so we know if it will rain. They help our phones work so we can talk to our families. And sometimes, they help countries see what's happening on the ground from very, very high up.

Looking Down from Space

Russia is very good at making and using these special 'eyes in the sky'. And it looks like Russia is helping Iran use them too! This is called 'satellite support'. Imagine having a super telescope that can see everything clearly from space! If Iran gets help with satellites, it means their army can see things better. Maybe they can keep a better eye on their borders to make sure their country is safe. It's like having a big magnifying glass that can zoom in on places from way, way up. This kind of help is very important for a country's protection.

A Little Help, Not Too Much

Even though Russia helps Iran with some special tools and 'eyes in the sky', there's something important to remember. Russia doesn't want to help *too* much more. The grown-ups say Russia 'lacks the will to do much more'. This means they don't want to give *all* of their best things or get involved in *everything* Iran does. It’s a bit like when you share your toys with a friend, but you don't give away your absolute favorite toy, right? Or you help your friend with homework, but you don't do all the homework for them. Russia helps Iran, but they also have their own plans and things to take care of.

Why Countries Work Together

Why do countries like Russia and Iran help each other? Well, sometimes they share common interests, which means they want some of the same things to happen in the world. Being friends and helping each other can make both countries feel stronger and safer. Did you know that countries often have special agreements with each other? These agreements are like promises to help or work together. It’s a bit like a big team working together to win a game.

Why This Matters to Us

Now, you might be thinking, "Why is this important for me, here in Africa?" Well, learning about what's happening around the world helps us understand how our big, beautiful planet works. Even though Russia and Iran are far away, it’s good to know about how different countries interact. It teaches us about friendship, helping, and how countries try to keep themselves safe. Just like you learn about your neighbors and how they live, we learn about countries far away. Every country is part of the big human family.

Keep Learning!

So, remember, Russia and Iran are two countries that help each other a bit with special army tools and 'eyes in the sky' called satellites. But Russia doesn't want to help too much more. It's a story of sharing and friendship between far-off countries! Keep asking questions, keep exploring, and keep learning, my amazing friends! The world is full of wonderful things to discover, and you are all brilliant explorers!
summary: Russia and Iran are two countries far away that help each other with special army tools and 'eyes in the sky' (satellites), but Russia does not want to give too much more aid.
whyMatters: Learning about how countries help each other, even far away, teaches us about how the world works, about friendship between nations, and how countries try to keep themselves safe.

originalTitle: Now in power, Nepal’s rapper-politician Balen Shah faces new challenge
title: Meet Balen: The Rapper Who Became a Leader!
content:
Hello, super kids! Are you ready for a really cool story today? It comes from a faraway land called Nepal. Imagine a country filled with tall mountains and amazing people. That's Nepal! And in Nepal, something very special happened. A man who loves music and helps people became a big leader! His name is Balen Shah. He is a very kind man. Balen has a big smile and a big heart. He lives in a city called Kathmandu. Kathmandu is the capital city of Nepal. It's a busy place with lots of homes and shops.

A Special Leader named Balen Shah

Before Balen was a leader, he was a famous rapper! Do you know what a rapper does? A rapper is like a storyteller, but with music! They speak words very fast, in a rhyming way, to a beat. It's like a poem you can dance to! Balen used his songs to talk about things that were happening in his city. He didn't just sing fun songs. Balen sang about how to make his city better. He sang about helping poor people. He sang about cleaning up the streets. His music was like a big message to everyone: "Let's make our city awesome!" Did you know that Nepal is home to the world's tallest mountain? It's called Mount Everest! It's so big, it touches the clouds!

Balen's Big Idea: Music and Helping!

Now, let's talk about leaders. We call them politicians sometimes. A politician is a person who helps run a city or a country. They make rules to keep everyone safe. They decide how to build new schools and hospitals. They work hard to solve problems for the people. It's a very important job! Balen wanted to be a politician. He wanted to use his voice, not just for music, but to make real changes. He wanted to help his city, Kathmandu, be the best it could be. He saw things that needed fixing, and he thought, "I can help fix that!" Most politicians in Nepal used to be older people. They spoke in a very serious way. But Balen was different. He was younger, and he spoke in a way that was new and fresh. He wasn't afraid to say what he really thought. He was very honest! Some people called Balen a "rebel." This means he did things in his own way. He didn't follow the old rules of how politicians should act or talk. He was direct and told people the truth. He used simple words everyone could understand. This made many people listen to him. Did you know that music can help people feel happy and even help them learn new things? It's like magic for your ears!

Winning the Hearts of Many

When it was time to choose a new leader for Kathmandu, many people voted. Voting means you pick the person you think will do the best job. Balen stood up and said, "I want to be your leader!" He told everyone his ideas. And guess what? Many, many people chose Balen! They liked his fresh ideas. They liked that he was honest. They liked that he cared about their city. So, Balen Shah became the new mayor of Kathmandu. The mayor is like the super-leader of a city! Everyone was so excited! It was a big day for Kathmandu. It showed that even someone different, someone who was a rapper, could become a great leader. It was a story about new beginnings.

Now, the Hard Work Begins!

Now that Balen is the mayor, his real work begins. He has a new "challenge." What does a challenge mean? A challenge is like a big puzzle you need to solve. It's a hard job that needs lots of thinking and hard work. His challenge is to make Kathmandu better. He needs to make sure there is clean water for everyone. He needs to make sure all kids can go to school. He needs to make sure the streets are clean and safe. These are all big jobs! Being a leader is not just about singing songs. It's about making tough decisions. It's about listening to people. It's about finding ways to help everyone in the city. Balen has to prove that his different way of doing things can really help. Did you know that a capital city is usually where the government of a country meets? It's like the main office of a whole country!

What Will Balen Do?

People have big hopes for Balen. They hope he will bring new ideas to solve old problems. They hope he will listen to everyone, even the little kids who have ideas. They hope he will make Kathmandu shine! Will Balen be a great leader? Will he fix all the problems? No one knows for sure, but everyone is watching with hope. He wants to show that being different can be good for leading. He wants to show that new ideas can help everyone. Did you know that many countries around the world have leaders who are musicians, artists, or even actors? They use their creativity to help people!

A Story of Hope for Everyone

This story of Balen Shah teaches us something important. It teaches us that it's good to be yourself. It teaches us that new ideas can be great. And it teaches us that anyone, even someone who sings songs, can step up and try to make their community a better place. Maybe one day, you will be a leader in your own way! You can help your family. You can help your school. You can help your friends. Every little bit of help makes a big difference, just like Balen is trying to do in Nepal. So, let's cheer for Balen Shah and his big adventure in leading Kathmandu! It's a wonderful story of music, new ideas, and trying to make the world a better place. Keep shining, Balen!
summary: Balen Shah, a famous rapper from Nepal, became the new mayor of Kathmandu by promising to do things differently, and now faces the big challenge of making his city better.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows that new ideas and different kinds of people can become great leaders, helping their communities in exciting ways.

originalTitle: Panic buying prompts PM to reassure Australians over fuel supply
title: Don't Worry, There's Enough Fuel! News from Far Away Australia!
content:
Jambo, my wonderful friends! Welcome to your special news report from Africa! Today, we have an exciting story from a land very, very far away called Australia. It's about something important that makes our cars, buses, and motorcycles go: fuel!

What is Fuel?

Fuel is like food for vehicles. Just like you need food to run and play, cars need special liquid to move. This liquid can be petrol or diesel. It's a very important kind of energy! Imagine your dad or mum driving the car to school or to the market. That car needs fuel to get there. Without fuel, the car cannot move, just like a hungry lion cannot run very fast! Did you know? The fuel we use often comes from deep, deep under the ground! It's like a special treasure the Earth keeps for us.

People Got Worried in Australia

Now, in Australia, some people heard stories that made them feel a little bit worried. They heard that maybe there wouldn't be enough fuel for everyone. This made them feel anxious. When people feel worried about not having enough of something, they sometimes do something called 'panic buying'.

What is Panic Buying?

Panic buying is when lots and lots of people rush to buy the same thing all at once. Imagine if everyone in your school suddenly decided to buy all the bananas from the market at the exact same time! Even if there were many bananas, they would all disappear from the market shelves very, very quickly. That's what panic buying looks like with fuel. People rushed to fuel stations to fill their cars, and also to fill extra cans with fuel. They wanted to make sure they had enough, just in case. Did you know? When everyone buys extra things at once, the shops can look empty even if there is plenty of stock kept in a big storage room!

The Leader Spoke Up!

Australia has a special leader, just like your country has a president or a chief. His name is Mr. Anthony Albanese. He is called the Prime Minister. Mr. Albanese saw that people were worried. He knew they needed to hear the truth. So, he spoke to all the people in Australia. He said, "Don't worry, my friends! We have enough fuel. Our fuel supply is secure!" What does "secure" mean? It means safe, strong, and that there is plenty of it. It means there is no need to worry because there is enough for everyone.

Why Was Everyone Worried?

Sometimes, people hear a rumour, which is a story that might not be true. Or maybe they saw a few fuel stations that looked empty because so many people bought fuel at once. This can make people think there is a big problem, even when there isn't. But Mr. Albanese, the leader, told them the good news. He told them that Australia gets its fuel from many places. It also has big places to keep fuel, like giant underground storage tanks. So, there is always more coming. Did you know? Leaders like Mr. Albanese work hard to make sure their country has everything it needs to keep going smoothly!

How Does Fuel Get Around?

It's very interesting how fuel travels! First, it is found or made in big factories far away. Then, it goes on huge ships called tankers that sail across big oceans. These giant ships bring the fuel to big ports in Australia. From there, big trucks, sometimes called fuel tankers, carry the fuel to all the different fuel stations across the country. So, even if one station looks a bit empty, more fuel is always on its way! It's like a very big and busy delivery system. Did you know? Some fuel tankers are so big they can carry enough fuel to fill hundreds of cars!

Why It's Good Not to Panic

It's always good to stay calm, even if you hear scary stories. When people panic and buy too much, it can cause problems for others who genuinely need things. For example, if a doctor needs fuel to drive to help someone who is sick, they might find the station empty because of panic buying. That would not be good! So, listening to trusted leaders like Mr. Albanese is very important. They have the right information and can help everyone understand what is really happening.

Good News from Australia!

So, my friends, the good news from Australia is that everything is okay with the fuel. Cars can still drive, buses can still take people to work and school, and life can go on normally. Mr. Albanese helped everyone understand that their country has enough fuel. So, the worries went away, and people could feel happy and safe again. Thank you for joining me for this news from around the world! Always remember to listen to the truth and not worry too much. Until next time, kwaheri! (That means goodbye!)
summary: In faraway Australia, people were worried there wouldn't be enough fuel for cars, but their leader, Mr. Anthony Albanese, told everyone not to panic because there is plenty of fuel for all.
whyMatters: This matters because fuel helps cars, buses, and trucks move, taking people to school and bringing food to markets. If people worry too much and buy extra, it can make things harder for everyone, so it's good to know there's enough.

originalTitle: Belarus leader gifts rifle to North Korea's Kim as they sign friendship treaty
title: Hello Friends! Leaders Make Big Friendship Promises!
content:
Hello Amazing Friends from Across Africa!

Hello, hello, hello! It’s your super friendly news reporter here, ready to share some really interesting news from far, far away! I hope you're all having a wonderful day, filled with sunshine and smiles. Today, we are going to learn about some very important people and some very important promises. It's like a story, but it's happening right now in our big, beautiful world! Did you know that our world is made up of many, many different countries? Just like your school has many classrooms, our world has many countries! Each country has its own special people, its own amazing animals, and its own unique stories.

Meeting New Friends: Belarus and North Korea

Today, we're going to talk about two countries you might not have heard of before. One country is called Belarus. Can you say 'Bee-lah-roos'? Great job! Belarus is a country that is very far from us, in a place called Europe. It’s a beautiful place with lots of forests and lakes. The other country we’re talking about is North Korea. Can you say 'North Koh-ree-ah'? Fantastic! North Korea is also very far away, in a place called Asia. It has big mountains and a very long history. Imagine traveling all that way – it would take many, many hours on a plane! Did you know? Africa has 54 different countries! That’s more countries than any other continent in the world!

Leaders Who Guide Their Countries

Just like your school has a head teacher who helps everyone, each country has a very important person called a 'leader'. This leader helps to guide the country, make important choices, and make sure everything runs smoothly for the people living there. The leader of Belarus is a kind man named Mr. Lukashenko. He has a very big job looking after his country. And the leader of North Korea is Mr. Kim. He also has a very big job guiding his country.

A Special Trip to Visit a Friend

Guess what happened recently? Mr. Lukashenko, the leader from Belarus, made a very long journey! He traveled all the way from Belarus to North Korea to visit Mr. Kim! Imagine flying for such a long time to see a friend. It shows how important their meeting was. When people travel to another country, especially a leader, it’s a very big deal! They don't just go for fun; they go to talk about important things and to build stronger friendships between their countries. Mr. Lukashenko went to a city called Pyongyang, which is the capital city of North Korea. A capital city is like the main office of a country, where many important decisions are made. Did you know? The longest flight in the world can take almost 19 hours! That’s longer than you sleep for two whole nights!

A Gift to Show Friendship

When you visit a friend, sometimes you bring a gift, right? Maybe a toy, or a yummy snack? Well, Mr. Lukashenko brought a special gift for Mr. Kim. It was a very unique piece of equipment. It was a way of showing warmth and respect, and that he wanted to be good friends with Mr. Kim and his country. Giving gifts is a very old and special way to show someone you care about them and want to be their friend. It's a symbol of peace and good feelings between people, and in this case, between countries. It's like saying, "I want us to be strong friends!"

Signing a Big Friendship Promise

But that’s not all! The two leaders, Mr. Lukashenko and Mr. Kim, did something even more important than sharing a gift. They sat down together and made a very special agreement. They signed a 'friendship treaty'. What is a friendship treaty? It’s like a super important promise that two countries write down on paper. They promise to be good friends, to help each other if they need to, and to work together on important things. It’s like when you make a pinky promise with your best friend, but much, much bigger and for entire countries! This treaty means that Belarus and North Korea are promising to be close partners. They want to share ideas and work together to make their countries strong and happy. It's a way for countries to say, "We choose to be friends and help each other grow." Did you know? The oldest known treaty in the world was made over 3,000 years ago between two ancient kingdoms! People have been making agreements for a very long time!

Why Friends Stick Together

Now, you might be wondering, why are these two countries becoming such close friends right now? Well, both Belarus and North Korea are also very good friends with another big country called Russia. Sometimes, countries like to stick together and help each other, especially when there are some big disagreements happening in the world. It’s like when you and your friends support each other during a game or when you need help with your homework. These countries are showing that they want to stand together and support each other's decisions. They are 'closing ranks', which means they are becoming even stronger friends and partners.

What This Means for the World

When leaders meet and sign treaties, it's always important news because it can change how countries work together. This new friendship between Belarus and North Korea means they will try to help each other more. They will talk more often and try to solve problems together. For us, it's a good reminder that friendship is important, not just between people, but also between countries. When countries are friends, they can share ideas, help each other, and sometimes even learn new things from each other. It helps to make the world a more connected place.

Learning from Our Leaders

So, what can we learn from this news? We can learn that friendship is powerful! Whether it’s between two people, or two big countries, being a good friend means supporting each other and making promises to help. It reminds us that even though countries might be far apart, they can still choose to be friends and work together. Keep exploring, keep learning, and keep being amazing friends to everyone around you! Until next time, this is your African kid-friendly reporter signing off! Bye for now!
summary: The leaders of Belarus and North Korea met, exchanged a special gift, and signed a big friendship promise (a treaty) to become closer friends and help each other, just like they are also good friends with Russia.
whyMatters: This is important because when countries become closer friends, they promise to work together, which can change how different parts of the world get along and help each each other with big decisions.

originalTitle: Trump's signature to appear on US dollars in first for sitting president
title: A President's Special Mark on US Money!
content:
Hello, amazing friends in Africa! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, ready with some super exciting news from far, far away! Today, we're going to talk about money, leaders, and a very special mark on some notes.

What is Money?

Do you know what money is? It's that special paper or shiny coin we use to buy things we need and love! We use money to get yummy food, cool toys, school books, and even pay for our homes. Every country has its own special money. In some places, you might use Naira, Shillings, or Rand. Money helps the world go 'round! When you buy something, you give money, and someone else gets it for their hard work. It's like a big swap! Countries also use money to build schools for you, make roads for cars, and help doctors and nurses.

Meet the US Dollar!

Today, our news is about the US Dollar. This is the money that people use in a country called America. It's a very big country far away, across the ocean. The US Dollar looks like special green and white paper, with pictures of famous people and numbers on it. Just like your country's money has special pictures, so does the US Dollar! Did you know? There are many different kinds of US Dollar notes! Some are small, like a 1 dollar note, and some are bigger, like a 10 dollar note or even a 100 dollar note! They all have important pictures and special designs to keep them safe.

What is a Signature?

Now, let's talk about signatures! A signature is like a special way of writing your name. It's not just regular handwriting. It's a unique scribble that only *you* make. Grown-ups use their signatures for important papers. It's like saying, "Yes, this is from me, and it's real!" Imagine you draw a beautiful picture. You put your name on it, right? That's like your special mark. Well, important people put their special marks, their signatures, on money too! It shows that the money is real and officially approved by the country.

Who is the President?

Every country needs a leader, right? Someone who helps make important decisions for everyone. In America, the top leader is called the President. The President is like the head of the big family that is the whole country! They work very hard to make sure things run well for all the people living there. The President has many big jobs. They help decide about schools, about staying safe, and about working with other countries. It's a very, very important job! Right now, the person who was the President of America when this news happened is named Donald Trump.

Who is the Treasury Secretary?

Okay, so we know about the President. But there's another important grown-up we need to know about. This person is called the Treasury Secretary. What does a Treasury Secretary do? Well, they are like the chief helper for all the country's money! They make sure the money is printed correctly and that it's safe for everyone to use. The Treasury Secretary works very closely with the President on all things related to money. They help decide how much money should be printed, and they make sure it's all handled properly. This person has a very big responsibility to keep the country's money strong.

Why Signatures on Money?

So, why do these important grown-ups put their signatures on money? It's super simple! It's like a stamp of approval. When you see their signatures, it tells you that this piece of money is real, it's official, and it's ready to be used. It helps people trust that their money is good. Think of it like this: if you get a birthday card, someone writes their name on it, right? It shows it's from them. Money works similarly. The signatures are like a sign from the top people in charge, saying, "This is good money!" Did you know? Not just any grown-up can put their signature on money! Only very specific and important people are allowed to do it. It's a special job for special people who look after the country's money.

A Special New Thing!

Now for the super exciting news! Usually, on US Dollars, you see the signature of the Treasury Secretary. And sometimes, you see the signature of someone who was a Secretary a long time ago. But this time, something new and very special happened! The President of America at the time, Donald Trump, also had his signature put on the new US Dollar notes! This is a really big deal because it's the first time that a *sitting* President, meaning the President who is currently in charge, has had their signature on the money while they are still President! So, the new US Dollars will have not one, but *two* important signatures: the signature of the Treasury Secretary, Scott Bessent, and the signature of President Donald Trump. It's like getting a double stamp of approval! This doesn't mean the old money isn't good anymore. It just means new money being printed will look a little different. It's a piece of history happening right now!

What Does This Mean for You?

You might be thinking, "But I live in Africa! Why is this important to me?" Well, it's super important to learn about different countries and how they do things! Learning about the US Dollar helps you understand that every country has its own special way of making and using money. Maybe one day you will travel to America, or you might see a US Dollar in a movie or book! Now you'll know a special secret about those notes – whose names are on them and why! It helps us understand the world better, and how different countries run their systems. Did you know? Some countries in Africa have very colorful money! And some even have pictures of wild animals like elephants or lions on their notes. Money around the world is full of amazing stories and art! So, next time you see money, whether it's from your country or another, remember all the special people who help make it and put their mark on it. It's a big job to make sure everyone has safe and good money to use!

Conclusion

Isn't that an interesting piece of news? A President's special mark on money! It's a small change on a piece of paper, but it tells us a big story about leaders and their roles. Keep learning, keep exploring, and keep asking questions about our amazing world! See you next time, my super curious friends!
summary: New US money will have the signature of President Donald Trump, which is a special first for a President who is still working.
whyMatters: It's important because it teaches us about leaders, money, and how countries work, helping us learn about the world.

originalTitle: Bellamy rues chaos against Bosnia as Wales' World Cup dream ends
title: Big Football Game: Wales Tries Hard, But Will Try Again!
content:
Hello, My Little News Friends!

Hello, hello, my wonderful friends! It's me, your kid-friendly news reporter from Africa! I hope you are having a super-duper day. Today, we have some exciting news from the world of football! Do you love playing football? Kicking a ball with your friends? It's so much fun, isn't it? Football is a game loved by many, many children and grown-ups all over the world. It makes us happy, it makes us cheer, and sometimes, it can make us a little bit sad. But that's okay, because we always learn and try again!

What Happened in the Big Game?

Today, we are talking about a big football game. It was a very important game for a team called Wales. Wales is a country far away, across the big ocean. Their football team played against another team called Bosnia-Herzegovina. These two teams played a special game. It was like a contest to see who could go to an even bigger football party! The game was tough! Both teams played with all their might. But, in the end, the team from Wales lost the game. Oh no! That can feel a bit sad when your favorite team doesn't win. But remember, playing the game is also very important. Trying your best is always a win!

What is the World Cup?

Now, let's talk about why this game was so special. It was for something called the "World Cup." Have you heard of the World Cup? It is the biggest football party in the whole wide world! Teams from many, many countries play in it. Imagine teams from Africa, Europe, Asia, and other places all coming together to play football! **Did you know?** The World Cup happens only once every four years! That's a long time to wait, isn't it? It's like waiting for four birthdays to pass before the next one comes around. Everyone gets very excited when the World Cup is near! Winning the World Cup means your country is the best football team in the world! Wow!

What are Play-off Games?

The game that Wales played was called a "play-off" game. What does "play-off" mean? It's like a special extra game. Imagine your school has a big sports day. Maybe only the top two teams get to go to the final race. If many teams want to go, they might play a special game first. This game helps decide which teams are strong enough to go to the very big competition. So, for Wales, this play-off game was their chance to try and get into the big World Cup party. They really wanted to go! But because they lost, they won't be able to play in the World Cup this time. It’s a little disappointing, but it's part of sports.

Who is Coach Craig Bellamy?

Every football team has a very important person called a "coach." The coach is like the team's teacher and leader. They help the players learn how to play better. They tell the players what to do on the field. They help them practice and become strong. The coach for the Wales team is a man named Craig Bellamy. He is a very smart man who knows a lot about football. After the game, Coach Bellamy talked about what happened. He was a little sad, just like his team. But he also said something very important and very kind.

The "Chaos" of the Game!

Coach Bellamy said that the game felt like "chaos." "Chaos" is a big word, isn't it? What does it mean? Imagine your toys are all over the floor, everywhere! Or maybe everyone in your classroom is talking at once, and it's hard to hear anything. That feeling of things being messy, not organized, and a bit confusing, that's what "chaos" means. So, when Coach Bellamy said the game was "chaos," he meant that things didn't go as planned for his team. Maybe the players found it hard to pass the ball. Maybe the other team played in a way that made it hard for Wales to play their best. Sometimes, in a game, things can get a bit messy, and it's hard to keep everything in order. But that's okay, because teams can learn from these moments.

But There's Good News!

Even though the Wales team lost and things felt a bit like "chaos," Coach Bellamy had a very happy message! He said, "my team have a bright future." Isn't that wonderful? What does "bright future" mean? It means he believes in his players. He believes that even though they lost this time, they will learn from it. They will practice even harder. They will get better and stronger. They will keep working together as a team. A "bright future" means there are many more good games to play, and many more chances to win in the future. It means they will not give up! This is a very important lesson for all of us. Even if you try your best and don't win a game, or you don't get the best mark on a test, it's not the end. You can always learn, grow, and have a "bright future"! Just like the Wales football team.

Did You Know? Fun Football Facts!

Here are some more fun facts about football! **Did you know?** A football game has two halves. Each half is 45 minutes long. That's a total of 90 minutes of running, kicking, and cheering! Phew, that's a lot of running! **Did you know?** There are 11 players on each team on the field at one time. One player is the goalkeeper, who tries to stop the ball from going into the net. The other 10 players try to score goals! **Did you know?** The World Cup trophy is made of gold! It is a very shiny and beautiful prize that the winning team gets to hold up high! **Did you know?** Football is sometimes called "soccer" in some countries, like America. But it's the same wonderful game!

Never Give Up!

So, my dear friends, even though the Wales team won't be in the World Cup this time, they are not sad for too long. Their coach, Craig Bellamy, knows that they have a "bright future." They will keep training, keep playing, and keep trying their best. And maybe, in the next World Cup, we will see them there! Remember, whether you are playing a game, learning something new at school, or helping at home, always try your best and never give up. Just like the brave football players from Wales! You all have a bright future too! Keep smiling and keep playing! See you next time for more news!
summary: The Wales football team lost an important game to Bosnia-Herzegovina, so they won't go to the big World Cup competition this time, but their coach believes they will learn and have a bright future.
whyMatters: This story shows us that even when we try our best and don't win, we can still learn, grow, and have hope for the future, just like a football team. It teaches us to never give up!

originalTitle: What next for Northern Ireland as World Cup wait continues?
title: What next for Northern Ireland as World Cup wait continues?
content: Manager Michael O'Neill says he is positive for the future despite a World Cup play-off defeat by Italy in Bergamo, and questions about his own long-term plans.

originalTitle: Managing in an international break - and what to do when your star player does not come home
title: Managing in an international break - and what to do when your star player does not come home
content: Tony Pulis explains why international breaks can be an opportunity as well as a problem for managers, and what he did when one of his players did not return on time.
